blocks.content: Zion looked back at Amy and sneered.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Hmph, serves you right. You should've known better who to mess with.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: You coward! Hurry up and untie me!

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Don't worry, I'll let you out soon enough. But first, enjoy the show.
Zeon half-turned and ordered Zenogre.

blocks.speaker: Zeon
blocks.content: Bring me Shirone's head.
Zenogre climbed up the spider web where Shirone was tied, wearing a sinister smile.
Using his mimicry ability to control his center of gravity, he moved freely across the steel wire without so much as a scratch.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Amy! Amy!
Even in this life-threatening situation, Shirone called out for Amy.
Seeing this, Zion felt even more delighted.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Although he wanted to torment Amy in front of Shirone, his desire to own Ataraxia as soon as possible was just as strong.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: 'Wait, but where's Arius?'
Everything was perfectly set up, so he should have appeared by now.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: He felt a flicker of unease, but it made no sense for a Scale Mage, who controlled time and space, to be late.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Most likely, he was planning to appear with a flashy entrance at the very last moment.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: 'Tch, always with the theatrics. Just can't help himself, can he?'
It wasn't like Zion didn't know his personality from the beginning.
Arius had his professional pride, so whatever happened, he would handle the job properly.
Zion looked around one last time and made a throat-slitting gesture.
Zenogre, looming over Shirone, licked his lips, extending his tongue like he was going to lick his face.
But Shirone, without even looking at him, mustered all his strength to shout toward Amy.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Amy! Run!

blocks.speaker: Zenogre
blocks.content: Kekeke, how pitiful. But you're going to die anyway.
Zenogre spread his arms and legs wide, positioning himself as if to crush Shirone.
There was a sound of something popping from his side, and two new arms emerged from inside his collar.
Shirone's face went pale at the sight.

blocks.speaker: Zenogre
blocks.content: Keke, what's wrong? Grossed out? I can grow up to six arms, you know.
Zenogre pulled steel wire from his mouth with his new arms and skillfully circled it around Shirone's neck.

blocks.speaker: Zenogre
blocks.content: There won't be any pain. I'm the best in the industry.
Sensing death, Shirone closed his eyes.
It seemed his short 18 years of life would end here.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Amy! Ig…!
Snip!
Before Shirone could finish speaking, Zenogre pulled the wire with both arms while laughing grotesquely.
The spider web dug into Shirone's skin, leaving a thin, ring-like mark on his neck.
Crackling sound of electricity from behind Zenogre.
It was the distinctive hum of Scale Mage's signature magic, Flicker.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: 'As expected.'
Zion clicked his tongue at Arius's antics.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: In any case, the timing was impeccable, so there was no reason to be angry.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Hm?
A puzzled look crossed Zion's eyes.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: It wasn't Arius. No, was it Arius? With the face covered by a mask, it was impossible to tell.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: What's with that get-up?
Without a word, the masked figure rolled forward and blocked the way to Reina and Amy.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: 'Not Arius? Then where is that bastard? Shirone is already dead...'
Zeon hurriedly turned his head towards Zenogre.
Although he wore his usual unsettling grin, his face now held a strange, subtly twisted expression, unlike anything one could normally see from him.
Shirone too had his eyes wide open while facing Amy.
And most importantly, the steel wire... remained frozen, slightly buried in Shirone's skin, as if frozen in time.
Crackling sound of Flicker Magic was heard.
As Arius materialized into the space, he tumbled across the ground, coming to a stop beside Zion.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Damn it! Am I late?

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: What! What's going on? Who is that person?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: I don't know. He's undoubtedly an Unlocker specializing in Time Magic.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: I tried to shake him off somehow, but they kept disrupting the coordinates...

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: 'Damn it. The compatibility is terrible.'

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: For someone specializing in spatial magic, a time specialist was their natural nemesis.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: No, then again, the reverse was also true—it was a relationship as opposed as fire and water.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: A relationship like fire and water where both parties are reluctant to accept each other.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Calculating the time to descend to first-stage primal consciousness...
Arius swallowed dryly.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: This wasn't over yet.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: There would be unconscious pressure since the brain wasn't completely separated from the body, but anyway, Shirone had received a near-death shock.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Furthermore, by the time he reached the first level, Shirone's condition would likely worsen.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: So as long as they secured a strong position, they still had a good chance.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Your Highness, I'm opening the Door.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: What? Now?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: There's still a chance. But as it happens, I will need some help. When I enter, please follow immediately.
Arius cast the Door Magic just outside Temporal Field.
As his Spirit Zone materialized, a two-meter-high oval sphere appeared.
Arius leaped inside, and Zion, grinding his teeth in frustration, hesitated for a moment.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Damn it! What a pain!
Zion withdrew his tentacles and dove into the door.
Only then did the masked figure unfold from their crouched position and stand up.
After thinking for a moment, they ran toward the Door without looking back.

blocks.speaker: Masked Figure
blocks.content: Wait!
The masked figure stopped at the door and looked back at Amy.

blocks.speaker: Masked Figure
blocks.content: Take me with you, too! It's like a gateway to Heaven, right? I've been there once. I can help save Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Are you sure? It'll be hot.

blocks.speaker: Masked Figure
blocks.content: Tha- That's… if you could just release me…

blocks.speaker: Masked Figure
blocks.content: Ignite.
Huh?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Don't you know Ignite? Aren't you a fire mage? Sticky spider webs are weak to fire. You could have escaped by burning it with flame magic.
Amy looked at the masked figure in astonishment.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Who was this person, who could cut only the web with a precise Wind Cutter?
Reina landed gracefully, then approached Amy.
She also looked at the masked figure with a puzzled look.
Though they didn't seem bad, she maintained her guard since their identity was unknown.
The masked figure examined the Door's condition.
Energy fields were radiating intensely from the edges of the portal—a sign that its time was running out.
